mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with an accuracy above 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth expectations and sustains positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 adjustments over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening cycles yields positive 20-day returns in over 58% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI decline below 3.0% confirms inflationary pressures are subsiding, allowing markets to price in imminent policy easing and generating immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain negative or flat in 65% of instances when CPI falls below the threshold, or the signal loses directional accuracy below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity and reduced consumer spending capacity, which systematically depresses corporate earnings forecasts and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.01, or forward 60-day returns show no negative correlation with payroll declines (Spearman rho > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth outpaces retail sales growth signals inventory restocking and pent-up demand, which resolves into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > RSAFS annualized growth
failure_condition: The GDP-Retail Sales spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical divergences, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production during a high-inflation regime forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, prolonging discount rate pressures and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth during high inflation yields positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 48% in walk-forward validation.
